_schema_docs.description: Technology tree nodes for bootstrapping industrial civilization from stone-age materials to semiconductor manufacturing. Replaces the former dependencies.json with a unified, hierarchy-aware structure that merges core phases and side quests into a single ontology.

hierarchy.domain: Top-level technology area (e.g., 'metallurgy', 'textiles', 'silicon'). Has no parent.
hierarchy.capability: Major skill or process family within a domain (e.g., 'metallurgy.smelting', 'textiles.spinning'). Parent is a domain.
hierarchy.process: Specific technique or operation (e.g., 'metallurgy.smelting.blast-furnace', 'textiles.spinning.drop-spindle'). Parent is a capability.

_schema_docs.id_format: Dotted hierarchical using kebab-case segments: 'domain', 'domain.capability', 'domain.capability.process'. The number of dots indicates depth: 0 dots = domain, 1 dot = capability, 2+ dots = process.

fields.id: Unique dotted hierarchical identifier. Must match the node's position in the tree.
fields.name: Human-readable display name.
fields.parent: ID of the parent node, or null for domain-level nodes.
fields.description: 1-3 sentence summary of what this node represents and why it matters.
fields.timeline: Estimated time range as a string (e.g., 'Years 0-10', 'Years 70-200+'). Inherited/approximate for capability and process levels.
fields.outputs: Array of product/material/capability tokens produced by this node. Referenced by edges in edges.json to define dependencies between nodes.
fields.critical: Boolean. True if this node is a critical-path enabler without which downstream technology is blocked.
fields.critical_note: String. Why this node is critical. Only present when critical=true.
fields.early_win: Boolean. True if this node provides a high-value output early, creating a positive feedback loop.
fields.early_win_note: String. Description of the feedback loop. Only present when early_win=true.
fields.pinnacle: Boolean. True if this node is an end-goal requiring the entire mature ecosystem plus generations of refinement.
fields.pinnacle_note: String. Why this is a pinnacle achievement. Only present when pinnacle=true.

notes: Outputs are orphan tokens — they become meaningful when edges.json (Task 3) connects them via 'requires' and 'produces' relationships.
The schema unifies former 'phases' and 'side quests' into a single hierarchy. No phase_type, phase_number, sq_number, or main_quest/side_quest fields exist.
Binary dependencies only (required/not) — no soft/optional dependency grading.
When populating (Task 6), ensure every capability.process parent chain resolves to an existing node.

nodes.name: Foundations
nodes.description: Food security, population growth, fire-making, charcoal production, stone and wood tools, basic pottery, and early kilns. The starting point for all civilization bootstrapping — no surplus means no specialist labor means no industry.
nodes.critical_note: Everything depends on food surplus to free specialist labor and charcoal for metallurgy
nodes.early_win_note: Charcoal + clay → better kilns → hotter fires → crucibles → enables metallurgy

nodes.name: Fire-Making & Charcoal Production
nodes.description: Friction fire, flint-and-steel ignition, and charcoal production via earth-covered pit and simple kiln methods. Charcoal is the primary fuel for all metallurgy until coke arrives. Every kg of iron requires roughly 1-2 kg of charcoal.

nodes.name: Stone & Wood Tools
nodes.description: Flint knapping (hard hammer, soft hammer, pressure flaking), ground stone tools, and wooden implements with hafting using birch tar adhesive. Produces scrapers, axes, drills, and digging sticks for all early construction and material extraction.

nodes.name: Agriculture & Food Production
nodes.description: Grain cultivation, animal domestication, food preservation (drying, smoking, salting, fermentation), and irrigation. The food surplus loop — more food → more specialists → better tools → more food — is the engine that drives all subsequent development.

nodes.name: Pottery & Kilns
nodes.description: Clay identification, processing, tempering, forming (pinch, coil, slab), and firing in simple updraft kilns reaching 800-900°C. Pottery provides containers for storage, transport, cooking, and critically refractory vessels for metallurgy and chemistry. Early refractory clay linings withstand 1200-1400°C.

nodes.name: Metallurgy
nodes.description: Extraction and working of metals from ore. Spans copper, bronze, iron, and steel production with increasingly complex smelting, casting, forging, and joining techniques. Also covers basic glass, lime, water and wind power, and spring making — the mechanical foundation for all machinery.

nodes.name: Copper & Bronze Production
nodes.description: Copper smelting from malachite, azurite, and chalcopyrite in forced-draft charcoal furnaces at 1085-1200°C. Bronze alloying with 5-12% tin for harder, more castable tools. Sand, clay, and stone mold casting techniques.

nodes.name: Iron & Steel Production
nodes.description: Bloomery smelting of iron ore at 1200-1400°C producing wrought iron, carburization for steel, and crucible steel (Wootz method). Heat treatment: annealing, quenching, and tempering. This is the critical metallurgical capability that enables all subsequent domains.
nodes.critical_note: Iron and steel are essential for machine tool beds, cutting tools, boilers, generators, and all precision machinery

nodes.name: Metalworking & Joining
nodes.description: Forge welding, brazing (brass and silver), soft soldering, and spring making. Anvils, hammers, bellows, and essential smithy tools. These joining techniques enable construction of complex mechanisms, watertight joints, and electrical connections.

nodes.name: Basic Glass & Lime
nodes.description: Glass production from silica sand + potash + limestone at 1100-1400°C, potash extraction from wood ashes, and lime production by calcining limestone at 900-1000°C. Glass for beads, vessels, and window panes; lime for mortar, whitewash, plaster, and hide processing.

nodes.name: Water & Wind Power
nodes.description: Undershot and overshot water wheels (1-5 HP), power transmission via flat leather belts and line shafts, and wooden peg gear trains. The primary motive power source before steam, driving grain mills, bellows, saws, and machine tools.

nodes.name: Machine Tools Bootstrap
nodes.description: Precision manufacturing capability built via Gingery-style iterative bootstrap: foundry → crude lathe → improved lathe → shaper → mill → drill press → surface grinder. The single most important parallel track — without machine tools, no semiconductor equipment can be built.
nodes.critical_note: Master enabler: every later stage depends on precision parts — cylinders, leadscrews, slides, seals

nodes.name: Charcoal Foundry
nodes.description: Crucible furnaces for aluminum (660°C) and cast iron (1200-1400°C), green sand molding with bentonite binder, pattern making with shrinkage allowance, and flask-based casting. Every machine tool starts as a sand-cast iron or aluminum casting.

nodes.name: Iterative Machine Bootstrap
nodes.description: The core self-improving sequence: crude lathe (0.5 mm tolerance) → improved lathe with leadscrew (0.1 mm) → shaper (0.05 mm) → milling machine (0.025 mm) → drill press → surface grinder (0.005 mm). Each machine doubles precision achievable with the previous generation.

nodes.name: Precision Metrology & Standards
nodes.description: Surface plates via Whitworth three-plate method, hand scraping, straight edges, squares, vernier calipers (0.02 mm), micrometers (0.01 mm), gauge blocks (Jo-blocks), go/no-go gauges, and thread standards. You cannot build what you cannot measure.

nodes.name: Bearings, Abrasives & Cutting Tools
nodes.description: Plain journal bearings (babbitt metal), rolling element ball bearings (52100 steel), natural abrasives (emery, sandstone) and synthetic abrasives (SiC, Al₂O₃ from electric arc furnace), cutting tool materials (carbon steel, HSS), thread cutting on lathe, and tap and die production.

nodes.name: Energy
nodes.description: Abundant, controllable power from coal and coke, steam engines, electromagnetic generation, electric furnaces, internal combustion engines, and electrolysis. The energy revolution transforms manufacturing from muscle-powered to machine-powered industry.

nodes.name: Coal & Coke Production
nodes.description: Beehive and by-product recovery coke ovens converting bituminous coal to coke (60-70% yield). Coke burns at 1800-2100°C with forced air — essential for blast furnaces and silicon production. Byproducts include coal gas, coal tar, ammonia, and benzene.

nodes.name: Steam Power
nodes.description: Newcomen atmospheric engine (5-15 HP, 0.5-1% efficiency), Watt separate condenser engine (10-100+ HP, 3-5% efficiency), boiler construction from riveted iron plate, safety valves, and power transmission from reciprocating to rotary motion.

nodes.name: Electricity Generation & Distribution
nodes.description: Voltaic piles and Daniell cells, wire drawing, generators (Faraday magneto → self-exciting dynamo), DC and AC motors, transformers for voltage step-up/step-down, and power distribution (AC wins long-term). Copper wire gauge reference and motor/generator sizing included.

nodes.name: Electric Furnaces
nodes.description: Electric arc furnace (EAF) for steelmaking and silicon reduction, submerged arc furnace for continuous production, and resistance heating elements (Nichrome, Kanthal, SiC). Arc temperatures reach 3000-3500°C. EAF is critical for MG-Si production and specialty steel.

nodes.name: Internal Combustion Engines
nodes.description: Otto-cycle piston engines (4-stroke, 5-65 HP), carburetors, magneto ignition, cylinder boring, crankshaft forging, valve trains, and cooling systems. Provides mobile power for transport and aircraft. Requires machine-tool precision boring and grinding.

nodes.name: Electrolysis
nodes.description: Electrolytic cells for chlor-alkali (NaCl → Cl₂ + NaOH), water splitting (H₂ + O₂), aluminum Hall-Héroult process, and copper refining. DC power at 2-5V per cell, industrial current 100-10,000+ A. Enables production of ultra-pure hydrogen, chlorine, and aluminum.

nodes.name: Advanced Welding
nodes.description: Oxy-acetylene welding and cutting (3100°C flame), arc welding (SMAW/stick) with flux-coated electrodes, and electrode manufacturing. Oxy-acetylene is the primary metal joining method from ~1900 until arc welding dominates ~1940.

nodes.name: Chemistry
nodes.description: Bulk mineral acids, alkalis, industrial gases, distillation infrastructure, and cement production. A civilization's industrial maturity can be measured by its sulfuric acid production. Provides the etchants, solvents, reagents, and purification infrastructure needed for semiconductor fabrication.

nodes.name: Mineral Acid Production
nodes.description: Sulfuric acid (lead chamber process 65-70%, contact process 96-98%), nitric acid (from nitrates + H₂SO₄, later Ostwald process), hydrochloric acid (NaCl + H₂SO₄ or direct H₂ + Cl₂ synthesis), and hydrofluoric acid (CaF₂ + H₂SO₄, extremely hazardous). HF is uniquely dangerous and essential for glass and silicon etching.

nodes.name: Alkali Production
nodes.description: Leblanc process (first synthetic soda ash) and Solvay process (more efficient, later) for Na₂CO₃, lime-soda process and chlor-alkali electrolysis for NaOH. Soda ash and caustic soda are essential for glass making, soap production, paper pulping, and chemical processing.

nodes.name: Distillation & Gas Handling
nodes.description: Distillation columns (2-10 m, copper or steel, packed with ceramic Raschig rings), batch and continuous flow reactors, piping/valves/pumps for corrosive service, shell-and-tube heat exchangers, gas compression and storage, and purification by scrubbing, molecular sieves, and catalytic getters.

nodes.name: Cement & Concrete
nodes.description: Portland cement from limestone + clay heated to 1450°C, ground with gypsum. Concrete (1:2:4 cement:sand:gravel mix), reinforced concrete with steel rebar, and curing protocols. Dominant building material for factory foundations, dams, roads, and vibration isolation pads.

nodes.name: Vacuum, Optics & Advanced Glass
nodes.description: Controlled environments and inspection capability. Vacuum pumps and chambers for deposition and evaporation, advanced glass production (borosilicate, fused silica, tubing), optical inspection tools (microscopes, comparators, spectroscopes), and precision temperature/pressure measurement.

nodes.name: Advanced Glass Production
nodes.description: Borosilicate glass (Pyrex-type, thermal shock resistant), fused silica/quartz glass (1700°C+, critical for CZ crucibles), glass tubing production, glassworking techniques (lampworking, sealing), and quartz crucible fabrication for semiconductor use.

nodes.name: Vacuum Technology
nodes.description: Piston pumps (rough vacuum 10-50 Torr), rotary vane pumps (workhorse, 10⁻²-10⁻³ Torr), diffusion pumps (high vacuum 10⁻⁶-10⁻⁸ Torr), vacuum chambers (steel or glass), vacuum measurement (Bourdon, Pirani, ion gauges), and leak detection (bubble test, helium leak detector).

nodes.name: Optics & Inspection
nodes.description: Lens grinding and polishing (rough grind → fine grind → polish with cerium oxide), compound microscopes (40x-1000x), optical comparators, spectroscopes, thermocouples, RTDs, pressure gauges, and electrical measurement (galvanometers, Wheatstone bridges). Essential for crystal defect inspection and lithography alignment.

nodes.name: Silicon
nodes.description: Production of metallurgical-grade silicon, purification to semiconductor grade, Czochralski crystal growth, wafer slicing and polishing, and fabrication of basic solar cells and semiconductor devices. Solar cells are the primary early target — far simpler than logic chips and creating a critical positive feedback loop.
nodes.early_win_note: Solar cells produce power to make more solar cells — the most important positive feedback loop in the entire tech tree

nodes.name: Metallurgical-Grade Silicon Production
nodes.description: Carbothermic reduction of quartz (SiO₂ + 2C → Si + 2CO) in submerged arc electric furnace at 1800-2100°C. Requires 5-30 MW power, produces 97-99% purity MG-Si. Energy consumption: ~11-13 kWh/kg. The starting material for all semiconductor-grade silicon.

nodes.name: Silicon Purification
nodes.description: Chemical purification via Siemens-like process (hydrochlorination → fractional distillation of chlorosilanes → CVD deposition, 9N-11N purity) and physical purification via directional solidification and zone refining (5-9N). Siemens process achieves electronic grade but consumes ~100-200 kWh/kg.

nodes.name: Crystal Growth & Wafering
nodes.description: Czochralski (CZ) crystal pulling from quartz crucibles with precision motion control, wire saw wafering, lapping (2-5 μm TTV), and CMP polishing (<0.5 nm Ra). RCA clean standard for wafer preparation. CZ puller is the most mechanically demanding equipment in the silicon path.

nodes.name: Czochralski Crystal Pulling
nodes.description: Pulling single-crystal silicon ingots from molten polysilicon using a seed crystal in a quartz crucible at ~1415°C. Requires precision leadscrew (±0.5% speed stability), argon atmosphere, and temperature control ±0.5°C. Dash process eliminates dislocations via thin neck growth.

nodes.name: Basic Semiconductor Devices
nodes.description: Solar cell fabrication (p-type wafer → phosphorus diffusion → SiNₓ AR coating → screen-printed metallization, targeting 15-18% efficiency), point-contact diodes, alloy junction transistors, and diffused transistors. Vacuum evaporation and screen printing for metallization.

nodes.name: Photolithography & IC Fabrication
nodes.description: Cleanrooms, photoresist chemistry, mask making, lithography exposure tools, and core fab processes (etch, deposition, doping, metallization). The planar process enables integrated circuits. Expect <1% yield on first IC runs — iteration is essential.

nodes.name: Cleanrooms
nodes.description: Cleanroom construction (ISO 6-4, HEPA/ULPA filtration, laminar flow, positive pressure, gowning protocols), ultra-pure water systems (RO + DI + UV + membrane filtration to 18 MΩ·cm), and cleanroom consumables (garments, wipers, gloves). A single 1 μm particle on a wafer can kill an entire chip.

nodes.name: Photoresists, Masks & Lithography
nodes.description: Photoresist progression from bitumen (100 μm+) to dichromated gelatin (10-50 μm) to novolac+DNQ (0.5-3 μm, positive tone). Spin coating, exposure (mercury lamp g/h/i-line), development in TMAH, and hard bake. Mask making via photoreduction. Contact/proximity and projection printing tools.

nodes.name: Core Fab Processes
nodes.description: Thermal oxidation (dry and wet), wet etching (HF for SiO₂, KOH for Si) and dry/plasma etching (RIE), CVD deposition (poly-Si, SiO₂, Si₃N₄), PVD (sputtering, evaporation), doping (diffusion and later ion implantation), metallization (Al, later Cu), and the planar process integration for IC fabrication. Example NMOS flow with 7 mask layers.

nodes.name: VLSI Scaling & Advanced Semiconductor
nodes.description: Continuous improvement of wafer size (2" → 12"), feature size (10 μm → 7 nm), layer count (1-2 → 10+), and yield (10% → 90%+). Advanced lithography (DUV, eventually EUV), ion implantation, ALD, CMP planarization, copper damascene interconnects, EDA tools, and high-end solar optimization. The pinnacle requiring a mature, self-sustaining industrial ecosystem.
nodes.pinnacle_note: Requires the entire mature ecosystem, generations of accumulated knowledge, and massive capital investment. EUV alone took 20+ years and $10B+ by ASML.

nodes.name: Continuous Scaling
nodes.description: Wafer size progression (2" → 4" → 6" → 8" → 12"), feature size reduction (10 μm → 3 μm → 1 μm → 0.35 μm → 0.13 μm → 7 nm), and metal layer progression (1-2 → 10+). Each generation doubles transistor count and requires better process uniformity, larger equipment, and higher yield.

nodes.name: Advanced Lithography
nodes.description: Projection steppers/scanners (i-line 365 nm → 0.35 μm), DUV excimer lasers (KrF 248 nm, ArF 193 nm with immersion → 0.07 μm), and EUV (13.5 nm from Sn plasma, reflective Mo/Si optics, high vacuum beam path). Chemically amplified resists for DUV. EUV is one of the last achievements — incredibly demanding.

nodes.name: Advanced Processes
nodes.description: Ion implantation (precise doping control), RIE and DRIE etching (Bosch process), atomic layer deposition (ALD, Angstrom-level control), CMP for global planarization, copper damascene interconnects with low-k dielectrics, multi-level metallization, and advanced solar cells (PERC, bifacial, fine-line metallization).

nodes.name: EDA, GPU Design & Advanced Packaging
nodes.description: Electronic Design Automation (logic synthesis, place and route, SPICE simulation, DRC), GPU architecture with billions of transistors, advanced packaging (wire bonding, flip-chip, chiplets, 3D stacking with TSVs), and software stack (drivers, compilers, libraries).

nodes.name: Knowledge Preservation & Education
nodes.description: Writing system development, paper production, movable type printing press, record keeping, technical drawings, education system (apprenticeships → trade schools → universities), library construction, and digital knowledge storage. Without systematic knowledge transmission across 50-200+ years, the entire effort dies with the first generation.
nodes.critical_note: Without knowledge preservation, the 50-200 year bootstrapping effort dies with the first generation. Every process recipe, ore location, and failure mode must be recorded.

nodes.name: Measurement, Timekeeping & Metrology
nodes.description: Base unit standards (length, mass, volume, temperature), timekeeping (water clocks → pendulum clocks → electrical clocks), precision instruments (micrometers, gauge blocks, go/no-go gauges), calibration infrastructure, electrical standards (Weston cell, standard resistors), and optical metrology (interferometry). You cannot build what you cannot measure.

nodes.name: Transportation, Logistics & Communication
nodes.description: Road construction (dirt → gravel → macadam → paving), bridges (timber, stone arch, iron truss), wheeled vehicles, canal transport, railways (steam locomotives), telegraph communication, and inventory/logistics management systems. Industrial-scale operations require moving materials, people, and information across distances.

nodes.name: Mechanical Computing & Automation
nodes.description: Manual calculation aids (abacus, slide rules, nomograms, mathematical tables), mechanical calculators (Pascaline, stepped drum, difference engine), automation (cams, linkages, governors, feedback control), automated machine tools (turret lathe, screw machine), and punch card data processing. Bridges the gap until electronic computers arrive.

nodes.name: Public Health, Sanitation & Medicine
nodes.description: Water supply and purification (wells, slow sand filters, chlorination), sewage treatment, hygiene and quarantine protocols, pharmaceutical production (plant-derived and synthetic), PPE fabrication, surgical capability, and gas detection. Skilled workers are the most valuable and irreplaceable resource.

nodes.name: Specialty Gases, Consumables, Packaging & Testing
nodes.description: Air separation (N₂, O₂, Ar), hydrogen production (electrolysis, steam reforming), silane production, dopant gases (PH₃, AsH₃, B₂H₆), etch gases (CF₄, SF₆, Cl₂, F₂), gas distribution systems (electropolished SS, MFCs), HEPA/ULPA filter production, cleanroom consumables, semiconductor packaging (die attach, wire bonding, encapsulation), and wafer probing and testing. The fab cannot operate without these.
nodes.critical_note: Hard blocker for semiconductor fabrication — ultra-pure gases, cleanroom consumables, and packaging infrastructure are prerequisites, not optional support

nodes.name: Air Separation & Bulk Gas Production
nodes.description: Linde fractional distillation of liquid air producing nitrogen (99-99.99%), oxygen (95-99.5%), and argon (99-99.999%). Argon is critical for CZ crystal growth inert atmosphere. Double-column distillation at cryogenic temperatures with Joule-Thomson cooling.

nodes.name: Hydrogen & Silane Production
nodes.description: Hydrogen via electrolysis (4-5 kWh/Nm³) with Pd membrane purification, steam reforming of methane, and silane (SiH₄) production from MG-Si via trichlorosilane redistribution. Silane is pyrophoric — rigorous handling protocols required.

nodes.name: Dopant & Etch Gases
nodes.description: Dopant gases (PH₃, AsH₃, B₂H₆ — extremely toxic, lethal at ppm concentrations) and etch gases (CF₄, SF₆, Cl₂, F₂). Fluorine production by KF·2HF electrolysis. Exhaust gas abatement via thermal oxidation and wet scrubbing mandatory for all dopant gas use.

nodes.name: Semiconductor Packaging & Testing
nodes.description: Wafer backgrinding, die singulation (diamond blade dicing), die attach (eutectic or epoxy), wire bonding (Au ball bonding, Al wedge), encapsulation (transfer molding with epoxy-silica compound), lead forming, wafer probing (DC parametric + functional), burn-in, and final test.

nodes.name: Energy Storage & Diversification
nodes.description: Lead-acid batteries, nickel-iron batteries, mechanical storage (pumped hydro, flywheels, compressed air), energy diversification (hydroelectric, wind, solar PV, biomass), grid infrastructure, and UPS systems for semiconductor fab equipment. Semiconductor fabrication cannot tolerate power interruptions.

nodes.name: Advanced Materials — Ceramics & Refractories
nodes.description: Refractory materials (fireclay, high-alumina, silica, magnesia, zirconia, carbon), technical ceramics (alumina, silicon carbide), ceramic processing (ball milling, pressing, sintering, slip casting), ceramic insulation (fiber blanket, insulating firebrick), and semiconductor-specific ceramics (fused silica crucibles, alumina furnace components, ceramic IC packages).

nodes.name: Textiles, Fiber & Cordage
nodes.description: Fiber cultivation (flax, hemp, cotton, wool), spinning (drop spindle → treadle wheel), weaving (frame loom → treadle loom), rope-making (hand-laid → rope walk), canvas and sailcloth production, drive belts for power transmission, filter cloth, and protective clothing. Civilization runs on string.
nodes.critical_note: Rope, cordage, and cloth are foundational for all pre-industrial infrastructure — mining hoists, tool hafting, power transmission, and worker protection

nodes.name: Lubricants, Oils & Fluid Mechanics
nodes.description: Animal fats and vegetable oils, lubrication regimes (boundary, hydrodynamic, EHL), cutting fluids for machining, grease production (calcium, sodium, lithium soap), bearing lubrication methods, hydraulic fluids, vacuum oils (mineral, silicone), and cleanroom-compatible lubricants. Without lubrication, every moving part seizes.
nodes.critical_note: Without lubrication, bearings gall, slides bind, gears wear out in hours, and cutting tools destroy both workpiece and themselves

nodes.name: Mining Engineering & Extractive Metallurgy
nodes.description: Surface and underground mining methods, rock breaking (hand tools, fire-setting, black powder blasting), mine ventilation, water pumping (manual → water wheel → Newcomen engine), hoisting and transport, and ore dressing (crushing, gravity separation, magnetic separation). Ore access is the hard constraint on every metallurgical process.
nodes.critical_note: Ore access is the hard constraint on every metallurgical process — the mine drainage → steam engine feedback loop drove all of industrialization

nodes.name: Petroleum & Alternative Chemistry
nodes.description: Petroleum extraction (surface seeps → cable-tool → rotary drilling), refining (batch distillation → fractional distillation → thermal cracking), coal tar chemistry (benzene, phenol, naphthalene from coke ovens), fermentation (ethanol, acetone, acetic acid, methanol), and wood gasification. Dual-path strategy: petroleum accelerates everything, but coal tar + fermentation covers the same molecules.

nodes.name: Aircraft Development
nodes.description: Internal combustion engines for aircraft (horizontally opposed twin, 20-65 HP), tube-and-fabric ultralight airframe construction, wooden propeller design and carving, doped fabric covering (nitrocellulose dope), and flight testing. Not on the critical path to semiconductors but dramatically expands logistics and reconnaissance.

nodes.name: Polymers & Composites
nodes.description: Natural rubber vulcanization, synthetic rubbers (nitrile/NBR, neoprene, silicone), thermosetting polymers (Bakelite, epoxy, phenolic/FR-4 laminates, polyester, polyurethane), thermoplastic polymers (polyethylene, PVC, nylon, polystyrene, PTFE), polymer processing equipment (extruders, injection molding), and composite materials (fiberglass, carbon fiber). Provides seals, gaskets, insulation, packaging, and structural materials.

nodes.name: Natural Rubber & Vulcanization
nodes.description: Latex collection (Hevea, guayule, Russian dandelion), coagulation, mastication on two-roll mill, compounding with sulfur, accelerators, zinc oxide, and carbon black, and vulcanization via compression molding or steam autoclave at 140-180°C. Produces gaskets, belts, seals, O-rings, and tires.

nodes.name: Synthetic Rubbers
nodes.description: Nitrile rubber (NBR — oil and fuel resistant, for gloves and seals), neoprene (weather/flame resistant, for wire insulation and adhesives), and silicone elastomers (PDMS, -60°C to +250°C, for high-temperature seals and electronic encapsulation). Nitrile gloves are critical for cleanroom and HF acid handling.

nodes.name: Thermosetting Polymers
nodes.description: Phenol-formaldehyde (Bakelite — electrical insulators, photoresist binders), epoxy resins (structural adhesive, die attach, IC encapsulation), phenolic/epoxy FR-4 laminate (PCB substrate), unsaturated polyester resin (fiberglass matrix), and polyurethane (foams, elastomers).

nodes.name: Thermoplastic Polymers
nodes.description: Polyethylene (LDPE/HDPE — films, containers, wire insulation), PVC (rigid pipe, flexible tubing, requires heat stabilizers), nylon (bearings, gears, engineering components), polystyrene (labware, insulation foam), and PTFE/Teflon (chemically inert, ultra-low friction, -200°C to +260°C, for seals and non-stick surfaces).

nodes.name: Composite Materials
nodes.description: Fiberglass production (E-glass fiber from silica/boron batch, 5-25 μm filaments), hand layup, vacuum bagging, resin transfer molding, and filament winding. Carbon fiber (PAN precursor, aspiration). Polymer processing equipment: extruders, injection molding machines, compression presses, and calenders.
